label: Bedbug bites with local reaction and no infection or anaphylaxis
inclusion_criteria: Adult with clinician diagnosis or discharge impression of bedbug bites with local skin reaction.
No anaphylaxis, systemic allergic reaction, secondary infection, systemic illness, high-risk host pathway, or uncertain bite diagnosis needing another pathway.
exclusion_criteria: Anaphylaxis, airway symptoms, hypotension, syncope, diffuse hives, or systemic allergic reaction.
Secondary skin infection, cellulitis, abscess, fever, systemic illness, immunocompromised host, or poor follow-up.
Tick, spider, scorpion, snake, or travel-related disease concern.
default_follow_up: Follow up with primary care, urgent care, or dermatology if itching is not improving, signs of infection develop, or bites continue.

source_audit.notes: CDC and EPA support bedbug bite framing, itch and rare allergic reaction risk, lack of known disease transmission, avoiding scratching to prevent secondary infection, and environmental infestation control. Autonomous Phase 1601-1650 approved this narrow v1 only for clinician-diagnosed bedbug bites with local reaction and no anaphylaxis, systemic allergic reaction, secondary infection, systemic illness, high-risk host pathway, or alternate dangerous bite concern.

review.notes: Reviewed as a narrow production ontology phenotype. CDC and EPA support bedbug bite framing, itch and rare allergic reaction risk, lack of known disease transmission, avoiding scratching to prevent secondary infection, and environmental infestation control. Autonomous Phase 1601-1650 approved this narrow v1 only for clinician-diagnosed bedbug bites with local reaction and no anaphylaxis, systemic allergic reaction, secondary infection, systemic illness, high-risk host pathway, or alternate dangerous bite concern.
required_modifiers: adult with clinician diagnosis or discharge impression of bedbug bites with local reaction
no anaphylaxis, systemic reaction, secondary infection, cellulitis, or systemic illness documented
no dangerous alternate bite or high-risk host pathway
